title: Definitions - Complaint
content: A 'complaint' means any allegation in writing made by a complainant that an unfair trade practice or a restrictive trade practice has been adopted by any trader or service provider; the goods bought by him or agreed to be bought suffer from one or more defects; the services hired or availed of or agreed to be hired or availed of suffer from any deficiency; a trader or the service provider has charged for the goods or for the services mentioned in the complaint a price in excess of the price fixed by or under any law, displayed on the goods or package, or displayed on the price list; goods which will be hazardous to life and safety when used are being offered for sale; or the trader or service provider has violated any consumer rights.

title: Definitions - Consumer
content: A 'consumer' means any person who buys any goods for a consideration which has been paid or promised or partly paid and partly promised, or under any system of deferred payment, and includes any user of such goods other than the person who buys such goods when such use is made with the approval of such person, but does not include a person who obtains such goods for resale or for any commercial purpose. A consumer also means any person who hires or avails of any service for a consideration which has been paid or promised or partly paid and partly promised, or under any system of deferred payment. The definition specifically includes transactions through electronic means, teleshopping, direct selling, or multi-level marketing.

title: Definitions - Defect
content: A 'defect' means any fault, imperfection or shortcoming in the quality, quantity, potency, purity or standard which is required to be maintained by or under any law for the time being in force or under any contract, express or implied, or as is claimed by the trader in any manner whatsoever in relation to any goods or product. The definition encompasses quality-related issues as well as deviations from claimed standards or contractual specifications. This broad definition ensures that consumers can seek redressal for any form of deficiency in the goods they purchase.

title: Definitions - Deficiency
content: A 'deficiency' means any fault, imperfection, shortcoming or inadequacy in the quality, nature and manner of performance which is required to be maintained by or under any law for the time being in force or has been undertaken to be performed by a person in pursuance of a contract or otherwise in relation to any service and includes any act of negligence or omission or commission by such person which causes loss or injury to the consumer. It also includes deliberate withholding of relevant information by such person to the consumer. This definition is broad enough to cover virtually any inadequacy in service delivery that harms the consumer.

title: Definitions - Unfair Trade Practice
content: An 'unfair trade practice' means a trade practice which, for the purpose of promoting the sale, use or supply of any goods or provision of any service, adopts any unfair method or unfair or deceptive practice including falsely representing that the goods are of a particular standard, quality, quantity, grade, composition, style or model; making a false or misleading representation concerning the need for or usefulness of goods or services; giving any warranty or guarantee of performance without adequate or reasonable test; disclosing to the public any information that is misleading; permitting the publication of any advertisement whether in any newspaper or otherwise, for the sale of goods or services at a bargain price when there is no intention to offer them at that price for a reasonable period; or refusing to take back defective goods.

title: Establishment of District Commission
content: The State Government shall establish a District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission in each district of the State, to be known as the District Commission. The District Commission shall consist of a President who shall be a person who is or has been or is qualified to be a District Judge, and not less than two members one of whom shall be a woman. The District Commission shall have the jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of the goods or services paid as consideration does not exceed one crore rupees. Every complaint shall be filed within the jurisdiction of the District Commission within whose local limits the opposite party resides or carries on business or has a branch office, or where the cause of action arises.

title: Jurisdiction of District Commission
content: Subject to other provisions of this Act, the District Commission shall have jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of the goods or services paid as consideration does not exceed one crore rupees. A complaint shall be instituted in a District Commission within the local limits of whose jurisdiction the opposite party or each of the opposite parties at the time of the institution of the complaint actually and voluntarily resides or carries on business or has a branch office or personally works for gain, or the opposite party or each of the opposite parties at the time of the institution of the complaint actually and voluntarily resides or carries on business or has a branch office or personally works for gain where the cause of action wholly or in part arises.

title: Establishment of State Commission
content: The State Government shall establish a State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission in the State, to be known as the State Commission. The State Commission shall consist of a President who shall be a person who is or has been a Judge of a High Court, and not less than four members one of whom shall be a woman. The State Commission shall have the jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of the goods or services paid as consideration exceeds one crore rupees but does not exceed ten crore rupees. The State Commission shall also have jurisdiction to entertain appeals against the orders of any District Commission within the State.

title: Jurisdiction and Transfer of Cases by State Commission
content: Subject to the other provisions of this Act, the State Commission shall have jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of the goods or services paid as consideration exceeds one crore rupees but does not exceed ten crore rupees and appeals against the orders of any District Commission within the State. The State Commission may, on the application of the complainant or of its own motion, transfer any complaint pending before one District Commission to another District Commission within the State if the interest of justice so requires. Every appeal shall be filed within a period of forty-five days from the date of the order of the District Commission.

title: Hearing by National Commission
content: The National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission, established by the Central Government under Section 53, shall have jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of the goods or services paid as consideration exceeds ten crore rupees. It shall also entertain appeals against the orders of any State Commission. The National Commission may on its own motion or on the application of the complainant transfer any complaint pending before one State Commission to another State Commission if the interest of justice so requires. The National Commission shall consist of a President who is or has been a Judge of the Supreme Court and not less than four members one of whom shall be a woman.

title: Appeal Against Order of National Commission
content: Any person aggrieved by an order made by the National Commission in exercise of its powers conferred by sub-clause (i) of clause (a) of Section 58 may prefer an appeal against such order to the Supreme Court within a period of thirty days from the date of the order. No appeal by a person who is required to pay any amount in terms of an order of the National Commission shall be entertained by the Supreme Court unless that person has deposited fifty percent of that amount in the prescribed manner. The Supreme Court may entertain the appeal after the expiry of the limitation period if it is satisfied that there was sufficient cause for not filing it within the prescribed period.

title: Establishment of Central Consumer Protection Authority
content: The Central Government shall, by notification, establish a body to be known as the Central Consumer Protection Authority to regulate matters relating to violation of the rights of consumers, unfair trade practices, and false or misleading advertisements which are prejudicial to the interests of the public and consumers. The CCPA shall consist of a Chief Commissioner and such number of other Commissioners as may be prescribed, to be appointed by the Central Government. The CCPA shall have the power to inquire or investigate into violations of consumer rights, pass orders to recall goods or withdrawal of services that are dangerous, hazardous, or unsafe, and issue directions to the concerned trader or manufacturer to reimburse the prices of goods or services so recalled to purchasers.

title: Product Liability
content: A product liability action may be brought by a complainant against a product manufacturer, a product service provider, or a product seller, as the case may be, for any harm caused to him on account of a defective product. This provision introduces the concept of product liability in Indian consumer protection law for the first time. A product manufacturer shall be liable in a product liability action if the product contains a manufacturing defect, is defective in design, if there is a deviation from manufacturing specifications, if the product does not conform to the express warranty, or if the product fails to contain adequate instructions of correct usage or warning regarding improper or incorrect usage. A product service provider shall be liable if the service was faulty or imperfect or deficient or inadequate in quality.

title: Liability of Product Manufacturer
content: A product manufacturer shall be liable in a product liability action where a harm is caused to a claimant on account of a defective product manufactured by such product manufacturer if the product contains a manufacturing defect, the product is defective in design, there is a deviation from manufacturing specifications, the product does not conform to the express warranty, the product fails to contain adequate instructions of correct usage to prevent any harm, or the product fails to contain adequate warnings regarding improper or incorrect usage. The product manufacturer shall not be liable if the product was misused, altered or modified by the consumer.

title: Mediation
content: The District Commission, the State Commission, or the National Commission shall, wherever it considers it appropriate having regard to the nature of the complaint, the availability and willingness of parties to submit to mediation, refer the matter for mediation. The mediation shall be held by a mediator nominated from the empanelled mediators of the respective mediation cell attached to each Consumer Commission. The mediator shall have regard to the rights and obligations of the parties, the usages of trade, the circumstances giving rise to the dispute, and such other relevant factors. If the mediation is successful, the terms of settlement shall be reduced in writing and signed by both parties. If mediation fails, the matter shall proceed before the Consumer Commission from the stage at which it was referred.

title: Offences and Penalties - False or Misleading Advertisement
content: The Central Authority may, after investigation, impose a penalty on a manufacturer or an endorser of a false or misleading advertisement which may extend to ten lakh rupees. For every subsequent contravention, the penalty may extend to fifty lakh rupees. The Central Authority may also prohibit the endorser of a false or misleading advertisement from making any endorsement for a period which may extend to one year. For every subsequent contravence, the prohibition may extend to three years. Where the Central Authority deems it necessary in the interest of the general public, it may, by order, prohibit the manufacturer or endorser of a false or misleading advertisement from accessing the media for such period as it may determine.

title: Punishment for Manufacturing, Selling, Storing, Distributing or Importing Adulterated Products
content: Where an adulterated or spurious good which is dangerous to human life is manufactured or sold or stored or distributed or imported, the offence shall be punishable with imprisonment which shall not be less than six months but may extend to seven years and with fine which shall not be less than one lakh rupees but may extend to five lakh rupees. Where such act results in any grievous hurt, the offence shall be punishable with imprisonment not less than one year but which may extend to seven years and with fine not less than three lakh rupees but which may extend to five lakh rupees. Where such act results in death, the offence shall not be less than seven years but may extend to imprisonment for life and fine not less than five lakh rupees but which may extend to ten lakh rupees.
